knowledge.domain_context: Brand identity ist nicht Kosmetik sondern eine strukturale Entscheidung mit weitreichenden Folgen. Eine starke Markenidentität constraint alle downstream Agents (Copywriting, Design, Social Media) mit klaren Richtlinien und verhindert inkonsistentes Marketing. Der Brand Agent fungiert als Single Source of Truth für alle Brand-bezogenen Entscheidungen. Ein schwach definiertes Brand Voice führt zu fragmentiertem Messaging, verminderter Kundenwahrnehmung und bis zu 33% Umsatzeinbußen. Der Brand Agent orchestriert neun Capabilities über vier strategische Ebenen: (1) Strategische Fundierung (Voice Architecture, Positioning, Naming), (2) Visuelle Systeme (Design Tokens, Color Psychology, Typography), (3) Audit & Governance (Consistency Metrics, Drift Detection, Asset Governance), (4) Dynamik & Evolution (Rebranding Strategy, Compliance, Founder Brand Integration). Alle nachgelagerten Agents konsumieren die Knowledge Outputs des Brand Agents als unveränderliche Constraints.

reference_docs.title: Brand Voice Architecting & Tone Matrix Pattern
topics: Tone Matrix Design: Dimensionen wie Education vs Empathy vs Expert
Context-to-Tone Mapping: Unterschiedliche Töne für verschiedene Kanäle (Email, Support, Product)
Voice Characteristics: Persönlichkeitstrait-Definition (z.B. 'Confident but not cocky')
B2B SaaS Spezifika: Technical Communication + Thought Leadership + Accessibility
Channel-spezifische Anpassung: LinkedIn (Authoritative) vs Twitter (Friendly) vs Support (Empathetic)
reference_docs.reference: Bigeye Ultimate Guide to Brand Voice Frameworks, Wynter Tone of Voice Guide, Foleon Brand Messaging

reference_docs.title: Visual Identity Systems & Design Token Engineering
topics: W3C DTCG Specification (v2025.10): Standard format for token exchange
Semantic Color Taxonomies: Base colors → Functional tokens (primary, secondary, danger) → Component-level overrides
Typography Progression: Font hierarchy via token-based scale (xs, sm, base, lg, xl, 2xl)
Design Token Scoping: CSS Custom Properties für themability, component-level isolation
Tailwind v4 Integration: Design Tokens → CSS Custom Properties → Utility-First Framework
Color Psychology: Kulturelle Konnotationen, Kontrast-Anforderungen (WCAG AA ≥4.5:1 Text, ≥3:1 UI-Elemente)
reference_docs.reference: W3C Design Tokens Community Group, Tailwind CSS v4 Architecture, Style Dictionary DTCG Integration, Tokens Studio, Terrazzo

reference_docs.title: Messaging Frameworks & Value Proposition Hierarchy
topics: Positioning Statement Template: '_______ for _______ who _______ because _______'
Value Prop Hierarchy per Segment: Primary (executive) vs Secondary (user) vs Tertiary (support)
Marty Neumeier Onlyness Test: 'Our _____ is the only _____ that _____'
Emotional Elevation: Funktionale Benefits + emotionale Storytelling (Debbie Millman)
Messaging Matrix: Persona-basiert (IT Director technical tone) vs Channel-basiert (LinkedIn authoritative)
reference_docs.reference: Marty Neumeier Brand Positioning, Debbie Millman Brand Thinking, Emily Heyward Obsessed Framework

reference_docs.title: Naming Science & Linguistic Screening Rules
topics: Phonetic Analysis: Aussprachefreundlichkeit, Soundability across languages
Linguistic Connotations: Bedeutungen in priority languages (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
Semantic Safety: Ungewollte Assoziationen, kulturelle Taboos
Trademark Pre-Check: Prior Art search, URL availability, social handle availability
Global Linguistic Screening: Native-speaker review für key markets
Legal Clearability: Secretary of State checks, jurisdiction-specific trademark availability
reference_docs.reference: Catchword Branding Naming Guide, Lexicon Branding Corporate Naming, Language Solutions Linguistic Screening

reference_docs.title: Brand Audit Patterns & Consistency Metrics
topics: Drift Detection Signale: Logo color deviation (0.5% RGB shift = invisible to eye, detectable by algorithm)
Compliance Rate Tracking: % assets following standards, time-series trending
Asset Categorization: Official vs Community vs Unofficial assets, version control
Consistency Scoring: Visual (logos, colors, typography) + Messaging (tone, language) + Experience (customer interactions)
Automated Auditing: AI-powered visual recognition, NLP tone analysis, predictive drift patterns
Business Impact: Revenue correlation (23-33% uplift with consistent branding)
reference_docs.reference: MADnext Brand Consistency Audit, YouScan Brand Audit Platform, Inspo AI Design Audit Tool, Brand Auditors

reference_docs.title: Rebranding Case Studies & Strategic Evolution
topics: Mailchimp (2018): Retention of 'weird and lovable' essence + mascot refinement + Cavendish Yellow palette
Airbnb (2014): Bélo symbol (heart + location + A), community belonging positioning, immersive research (13 cities, 120 interviews)
Slack (2021): Simpler color palette, scalability across platforms, evolution vs revolution approach
Pattern: What Stays (core purpose, audience connection) vs What Changes (visual language, tone emphasis)
Research-Driven Process: Deep customer immersion, stakeholder alignment, phased rollout
Warning Signs: Brand drift without strategy, forced personality changes, loss of brand equity
reference_docs.reference: Brand New Archive, Strawberry Brand Studio Case Studies, Geek Boutique Airbnb Rebrand Analysis

reference_docs.title: Design Token Engineering & CSS Custom Properties Scoping
topics: Token Hierarchy: Design Time (figma/tokens.studio) → Source of Truth (JSON) → Runtime (CSS Custom Properties)
Scoping Strategies: Root-level (global) vs Component-level (scoped) vs Dark mode (pseudo-class override)
Tailwind v4 Integration: @theme directive, arbitrary values, token-aware utilities
Variable Naming Conventions: --color-primary-500, --spacing-xs, --typography-base-size
Sync Workflows: Token Studio → JSON export → Tailwind config generation
Build Pipeline: Linting tokens (invalid colors, missing references), generating type definitions
reference_docs.reference: W3C DTCG Format Module 2025.10, Tailwind CSS v4 Documentation, Style Dictionary, Tokens Studio

reference_docs.title: Founder Brand Dynamics & Parasocial Relationship Architecture
topics: Why Founder Voice Scales B2B SaaS: Authenticity signals, trust building, perceived competence
Parasocial Relationship Mechanics: One-way intimacy, illusion of mutual recognition, psychological vulnerability
Authenticity Requirements: True belief in product, personal story sharing, consistent voice
Ethical Boundaries: Oversharing risks, responsibility ethics, audience psychological exploitation concerns
Integration Strategy: Founder voice as brand voice extension (nicht Replacement), consistency across platforms
Risk Mitigation: Succession planning bei Founder exit, brand resilience post-founder
reference_docs.reference: Parasocial Relationships in Branding (Winsome Marketing), Founder-Led Marketing (Great Entrepreneurs), Personal Brand SaaS (Nowspeed)

reference_docs.title: GDPR Compliance & EU Data Privacy for Brand Data
topics: Scope: Applies to any organization collecting EU resident data (email, IP, cookies, analytics)
Seven Core Principles: Lawfulness/Fairness, Purpose Limitation, Data Minimization, Accuracy, Storage Limitation, Integrity/Confidentiality, Accountability
Brand Data Specifics: Customer list compliance, research data storage, consent-based personalization
Consent Architecture: Explicit opt-in (nicht implied), withdrawal mechanism, purpose clarity
Third-party Vendor Risk: CDN data flows, analytics providers, SaaS tools must have data processor agreements (DPA)
Penalties: EUR 20M or 4% global turnover (whichever higher) for processing violations
EU Compliance Constraint (ADR-001): No US-hosted analytics, self-hosted assets, no Google Fonts CDN
reference_docs.reference: GDPR.eu Official, GDPR-Info.eu Legal Text, European Commission Data Protection, Ketch GDPR Compliance Guide

reference_docs.title: Brand Architecture & Portfolio Strategy (David Aaker Framework)
topics: Brand Relationship Spectrum: Branded House vs Subbrands vs Endorsed brands vs House of Brands
Five Dimensions: Brand portfolio, Portfolio roles, Product-market context, Portfolio structure, Portfolio graphics
Identity vs Positioning: Identity (broad, durable core) vs Positioning (context-specific emphasis)
Sub-brand Strategy: Additional layer of meaning to master brand (Tesla → Tesla Model S → Plaid)
Endorsed Brand: Parent brand provides assurance without direct ownership (Google → Google Cloud)
Portfolio Optimization: Synergy realization, resource allocation, growth enablement, clarity
reference_docs.reference: David Aaker Brand Vision Model (How Brands Are Built), Aaker Brand Architecture Framework, How to Build Strong Brands (Aaker/Joachimsthaler)

reference_docs.title: EU Compliance Constraints & Self-Hosted Asset Architecture (ADR-001)
topics: Europäische Dienstleister Only: No US-based hosting, AWS/Azure multi-region acceptable only if EU data residency
Self-Hosted Static Assets: Fonts, icons, images stored on EU infrastructure
Analytics Compliance: No Google Analytics (privacy concerns), use Plausible/Fathom/Matomo
WCAG 2.1 AA Minimum: Contrast ≥4.5:1 text, ≥3:1 UI elements, visible focus rings, semantic HTML
Skip-Link Pattern: Zum Hauptinhalt
Language Tag: <html lang='de'> für Accessibility + SEO
Third-party Vendor Audit: All integrations must respect GDPR + EU data residency
reference_docs.reference: ADR-001 EU Compliance Design Document, WCAG 2.1 AA Guidelines, GDPR Official

examples.title: B2B SaaS Email Campaign Tone Matrix Example
examples.scenario: Newsletter for software engineers (Product Hunt audience) vs CFO audience on same SaaS platform

product_hunt_engineers.do: Use jargon (API rate-limiting, latency), explain benefits via technical depth, reference open-source culture
product_hunt_engineers.dont: Avoid corporate speak, oversimplify technical concepts, use generic B2B clichés
product_hunt_engineers.example_subject: Latency dropped 40% with our new query optimizer (benchmarks inside)

cfo_audience.do: Lead with cost savings/efficiency metrics, reference compliance, use financial language
cfo_audience.dont: Deep technical jargon, casualness, unsubstantiated claims
cfo_audience.example_subject: Customers report 28% infrastructure cost reduction in Year 1

examples.title: Design Token Semantic Hierarchy for Tailwind v4
examples.scenario: Building a design system for an EU-compliant healthcare SaaS with dark mode support

token_structure.wcag_compliance: --color-text: 'var(--color-slate-900)' in light mode (contrast 16:1), --color-text: 'var(--color-slate-50)' in dark mode (contrast 16:1)

examples.title: Onlyness Statement & Positioning for Early-Stage Fintech
examples.scenario: Fintech startup competing against Stripe, PayPal, Square for European merchant payments

solution.onlyness_statement: Our payment infrastructure is the only platform built for EU regulatory-first businesses that want compliance + developer experience

value_prop_hierarchy.executive_cfo: Reduces compliance audit time by 60% (GoBD-compliant audit logs out of the box)
value_prop_hierarchy.developer: Single API for EU VAT handling, reverse-charge logic, SEPA payments
value_prop_hierarchy.support_team: Pre-built regulatory docs, audit trail transparency, customer support templates

positioning_differences.vs_stripe: We specialize in EU-specific regulations; Stripe is global-first
positioning_differences.vs_paypal: We prioritize developer experience; PayPal prioritizes consumer adoption
positioning_differences.our_slot: EU-native infrastructure for growth-stage commerce companies

examples.title: Rebranding Strategy: Evolving Without Breaking Brand Equity
examples.scenario: 5-year-old SaaS product with strong brand equity, user-generated content relying on old logo

what_stays.purpose: Help remote teams collaborate without friction

what_changes.visual_language: Modernize logo for scalability (app icon 16x16px requirement)
what_changes.color_palette: Deepen brand colors (WCAG AA 4.5:1 to 7:1 for primary CTA)
what_changes.messaging_emphasis: Shift from 'simple collaboration' to 'compliant collaboration for regulated industries'

solution.rollout_strategy: Phase 1: Launch new visual system on website + product (30 days), Phase 2: Community migration guide (60 days), Phase 3: Deprecate old assets

examples.title: Naming Linguistic Screening & Trademark Clearance Process
examples.scenario: German fintech startup choosing a name for international expansion (3-letter domain ideal)

phonetic_screening: ZENTZ: Easy in DE/EN, hard in Spanish (lisp required), risky in Italian (sounds dated)
NEXO: Neutral phonetically across languages, BUT 'nexo' = 'link' in Portuguese (no conflict)

semantic_analysis.zentz: No negative connotations found in priority languages
semantic_analysis.nexo: Potential German phonetic similarity to 'Nächte' (nights) — low risk

trademark_clearance: USPTO search: 'NEXO' appears 47 times, no class 36 (financial) conflicts
EUIPO search: 'NEXO' registered in class 42 (software) by Spanish company, class 36 clear
Domain: nexo.com available, nexo.de available, nexo.eu available
process.recommendation: NEXO — clear trademark path, global phonetic viability, available domains, semantic clean

examples.title: Brand Audit: Drift Detection in Real Action
examples.scenario: SaaS company with 18-month old brand system, scaling across 40+ marketing assets

color_drift.impact: 12% of assets outside tolerance (±2 points RGB acceptable)

tone_drift.support_emails: Overly formal corporate tone (should be empathetic, spec: 80% empathetic, 15% solution-focused)

recommendations: Immediate: Regenerate all PDF templates with color palette automation
30 days: Audit all paid media creative, migrate to brand asset library
60 days: Email template refresh with tone guidelines enforcement

examples.title: Founder Brand Integration: CEO LinkedIn Presence as Brand Extension
examples.scenario: B2B SaaS founder known for thought leadership, but personal brand doesn't directly reinforce company brand

problem.founder_brand: Technical deep-dives on distributed systems, cybersecurity research
problem.company_brand: Accessibility-first, user-centric design for non-technical SMBs

integration_strategy: Founder continues expertise content BUT with company context
Content bridge: 'How distributed systems thinking improves product reliability for small businesses'
Personal storytelling: Share onboarding feedback, customer success stories (anonymized)
solution.parasocial_authenticity: Founder recognizes community members by name, responds personally to questions, admits product limitations publicly
solution.risk_mitigation: Establish founder-independent brand identity (company logo, mission statement, team profiles) for succession planning
solution.measurement: Track: Company mentions in founder content, follower crossover (LinkedIn → Product signup), brand sentiment on founder posts

examples.title: GDPR Compliance Audit: Brand Data Collection & Third-Party Vendor Risk
examples.scenario: German SaaS company using Segment for analytics, Figma for design collaboration, Auth0 for authentication

data_flows.risk: HIGH — Segment free tier sends data to Google Analytics (US-based)
data_flows.fix: Switch to Plausible Analytics (EU-based, GDPR native)

data_flows.risk: MEDIUM — Figma uses AWS US regions by default
data_flows.fix: Request EU-only workspace, verify in Figma settings

data_flows.risk: MEDIUM — Auth0 default EU tenants available, but check DPA
data_flows.fix: Execute data processor agreement (DPA), confirm EU data residency

consent_architecture.before: Cookie banner with single 'Accept All' button
consent_architecture.after: Granular consent: (1) Essential (always on), (2) Analytics (opt-in, Plausible only), (3) Marketing (opt-in, no third-party pixels)
consent_architecture.withdrawal: Add 'Manage Cookies' link in footer, allow real-time preference change

compliance_inventory.audit_timeline: 30 days: DPA audit all vendors, 60 days: Migrate analytics, 90 days: Consent implementation

few_shot_prompts.context: User: 'We're launching a B2B SaaS for freelancers (like FREA). How do we define our brand voice?'
approach: Step 1: Understand customer profile — Freelancers range from technical (developers) to non-technical (designers, copywriters). They're independent, value autonomy, often frustrated with corporate tools.
Step 2: Create tone matrix
Education tone (documentation, tutorials): Clear, jargon-minimized, step-by-step (for non-technical freelancers)
Empathy tone (support, onboarding): Acknowledgment of pain points ('invoicing is tedious'), supportive language, solution-focused
Expert tone (thought leadership blog): Data-backed insights on freelancer economics, tax regulations, EU compliance
Step 3: Constraint all downstream teams
Copywriting: Blog post MUST include empathy + education (never just feature lists)
Support: Response template includes acknowledgment phrase before solution
Design: Friendly but professional (no corporate mascots, no cutesy illustrations)

few_shot_prompts.context: User: 'How do I ensure my design tokens are WCAG compliant and support dark mode?'
approach: Step 1: Define color semantics (not just 'blue', 'red')
--color-success: must be 4.5:1 contrast against --color-surface-default
--color-error: must be 4.5:1 contrast for small text, 3:1 for UI components
Step 2: Create token pairs for light/dark
Light: --color-text: #1a202c (slate-900, contrast 16:1 on white)
Dark: --color-text: #f1f5f9 (slate-50, contrast 16:1 on slate-950)
Step 3: Validate in Figma + CSS
Export tokens JSON → Run WCAG validator → Ensure all combinations pass
Test in Tailwind: @theme { colors { text: 'var(--color-text)' } } + test with dark: prefix

few_shot_prompts.context: User: 'A competitor just launched a rebrand. Should we rebrand too?'
approach: Step 1: Audit your current brand equity
What % of customers recognize your logo without text?
Does your brand voice differentiate you in customer feedback?
Run consistency audit: How much drift has accumulated?
Step 2: Define rebranding trigger
Rebranding reason: Are we pivoting market position? Merger? Loss of brand equity? No. → Don't rebrand.
If yes: Define what stays (purpose, values, core audience) vs what changes (visual language, tone emphasis)
Step 3: Plan phased rollout
Phase 1 (30 days): Website + product UI only → Gather feedback
Phase 2 (60 days): Marketing assets + community guidelines
Phase 3 (90 days): Deprecation timeline for old assets

few_shot_prompts.context: User: 'I've hired 10 agencies to run brand initiatives (naming, design, copy, social). How do I ensure consistency?'
approach: Step 1: Create a brand guidelines document (living, version-controlled)
Section 1: Brand voice matrix (tone dimensions per channel)
Section 2: Visual system (color tokens, typography scale, spacing grid)
Section 3: Naming rules (linguistic screening results, phonetic guidelines)
Section 4: Messaging templates (positioning statement, value prop per segment)
Step 2: Implement constraint layers
Layer 1: Brand agent outputs are non-negotiable (other agents cannot override)
Layer 2: Design tokens feed into Tailwind config → Designers cannot pick arbitrary colors
Layer 3: Copywriting template library (with voice tone examples) → Must be reviewed before publishing
Step 3: Monthly drift audits
Automated visual audit (logo color detection, typography analysis)
Manual tone audit (sample messaging, check for voice consistency)
Report back to brand agent for course correction

few_shot_prompts.context: User: 'We're expanding to EU markets. What brand/compliance risks should we anticipate?'
approach: Step 1: Map compliance constraints
EU Hosting: Ensure website + all services on EU infrastructure (GDPR data residency)
Self-Hosted Assets: Move fonts/images from Google CDN to self-hosted (privacy + GDPR)
Analytics: Replace Google Analytics with Plausible/Matomo (GDPR-native)
Step 2: Audit brand assets for cultural fit
Linguistic drift: Does brand name/slogan have unintended meaning in target language? (Run through native speakers)
Visual drift: Do colors have cultural implications? (Red = warning in Western cultures, luck in Eastern cultures)
Step 3: WCAG AA audit (mandatory for EU public sector + increasingly expected for all)
Contrast: Verify all text 4.5:1 minimum
Focus rings: Visible on all interactive elements
Semantic HTML: Proper heading hierarchy, form labels, navigation landmarks

few_shot_prompts.context: User: 'My founder is becoming the face of the brand on social media. How do we scale this without losing authenticity?'
approach: Step 1: Define founder brand integration
Question: Does founder personal brand reinforce or distract from company mission?
If reinforce (founder = expert in core competency): Integrate → founder content becomes company thought leadership
If distract (founder = investor thoughts, personal politics): Separate → founder has profile, company has separate profile
Step 2: Establish authenticity guardrails
Founder should only post about things they genuinely use/believe
Share product feedback publicly (even negative) to build credibility
Respond personally to comments (parasocial relationship building)
Step 3: Plan founder-independence
Build company brand identity that doesn't rely on founder
Document brand voice + messaging in guides (not in founder's head)
Develop team members as secondary brand voices (reduces single-point-of-failure)
Timeline: After founding, shift gradually toward 'founder + team' brand
